segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Zendaya, seated on a black couch in a warmly lit, plant-filled room, wearing a tan utility jumpsuit with her hair pulled back. She engages with an open book on a round wooden table, surrounded by decorative elements like colorful flowers, an hourglass, and a bell. Zendaya's voice immediately begins, reflecting on a past look, stating, "I still see memes about this particular look with my hat. But you know what? I stick by it." She then introduces herself, "Hello Vogue, I'm Zendaya and this is my Life in Looks," as the camera alternates between close-ups of her expressive face and wider shots of the setting. An upbeat, slightly retro background music track begins as the scene transitions into a dynamic montage of vintage-style video screens displaying fragmented clips of her past appearances, overlaid with glitch effects. Zendaya's voice returns, asking, "Alright, shall we begin?" as the narrative shifts to a scrapbook-style presentation. As pages flip, revealing a 2011 photo of her at the 9th Annual Teen Vogue’s Young Hollywood Party in a blue patterned dress, she comments, "Yeah, this is me and Law's early, early work together. I needed a cute little dress to go to my Teen Vogue party. Was a big deal. I'm like what, like 15 years old. So, I think I did alright." The scrapbook continues to a 2013 image from the 20th Annual Keep A Child Alive, Dream Halloween event, showing her in a red jacket and jeans. Zendaya explains, "This was a charity event, it was Halloween themed, so you were supposed to come dressed as somebody or something and I went dressed as Aaliyah, 'cause old Tommy Hilfiger was her look and her thing and so this was my interpretation of that. I did my little swoop and that was my tribute to the one and only." Additional nostalgic photos appear, including a younger Zendaya in a yellow jacket with sunglasses, and another in a blue jacket with a striped top. The segment concludes with a 2014 image from the 12th Annual Teen Vogue Young Hollywood Party, where she wears a sleek black jumpsuit and bowler hat, standing by a pool with purple lighting, as Zendaya's voice is heard saying, "This, this is a controversial look." Throughout, the atmosphere blends personal reflection with a curated visual journey through her fashion evolution, emphasizing her growth and style milestones, all underscored by her direct, conversational commentary.

segments.integrated_caption: The video transitions from Zendaya's elegant red carpet appearance in a shimmering, floral-patterned, one-shoulder gown at the 2024 96th Annual Academy Awards. Her voiceover explains this look was "just kind of taking it easy, going old Hollywood and just keeping it simple" after "all these very heightened looks during the Dune press tour." The scene then shifts to a cozy, plant-filled indoor setting where Zendaya, in a tan utility jumpsuit with her hair pulled back, is seated on a black couch. She interacts with a scrapbook on a round stone table, flipping through pages that display images from her 2023 Dune: Part Two premiere and her 2024 Academy Awards look. As she turns to a 2024 Vogue US May Issue cover featuring her lying amidst greenery, wrapped in a large red rose, with the headline 'Zendaya' and the tagline 'What does my future look like?', she states, "This is my most recent Vogue cover. This was really special because I got to shoot with Annie." Reacting expressively with hand gestures, Zendaya describes Annie Leibovitz as "so fun to shoot with," noting her "beautiful references for poses" and inspiration from "movement and dance." She further explains Annie's specificity and the challenge of "figuring how to move in these looks," some of which were couture garments that "hadn't gone down the runway yet," expressing concern about damaging them. She humorously imitates Annie's energetic direction, concluding that they "just tried to have fun and give these garments a different feeling with movement." With gentle, reflective background music playing, Zendaya then picks up a pen and begins to draw a stylized 'Z' in the scrapbook, revealing, "I made this signature when I was like 13," and finally identifying it as "a shooting star," suggesting a personal reflection on her image and legacy in the warm, intimate, plant-filled environment.

The music continues softly throughout the credits, which culminate in the iconic 'VOGUE' logo in bold white letters on a black background, signaling the end of the segment, with the music fading out.
